name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا رُسُلَنَا بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَأَنزَلْنَا مَعَهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ لِيَقُومَ ٱلنَّاسُ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۖ وَأَنزَلْنَا ٱلْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَنَـٰفِعُ لِلنَّاسِ وَلِيَعْلَمَ ٱللَّهُ مَن يَنصُرُهُۥ وَرُسُلَهُۥ بِٱلْغَيْبِ ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ قَوِىٌّ عَزِيزٌ (٢٥)
```

```english
Certainly We sent Our apostles with manifest proofs, and We sent down with them the Book and the Balance, so that mankind may maintain justice; and We sent down iron, in which there is great might and uses for mankind, and so that Allah may know those who help Him and His apostles [with faith] in the Unseen. Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty. (25)
```

```arabic
وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا وَإِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِى ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا ٱلنُّبُوَّةَ وَٱلْكِتَـٰبَ ۖ فَمِنْهُم مُّهْتَدٍ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (٢٦)
```

```english
Certainly We sent Noah and Abraham and We ordained among their descendants prophethood and the Book. Some of them are [rightly] guided, and many of them are transgressors. (26)
```

```arabic
ثُمَّ قَفَّيْنَا عَلَىٰٓ ءَاثَـٰرِهِم بِرُسُلِنَا وَقَفَّيْنَا بِعِيسَى ٱبْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ ٱلْإِنجِيلَ وَجَعَلْنَا فِى قُلُوبِ ٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً وَرَهْبَانِيَّةً ٱبْتَدَعُوهَا مَا كَتَبْنَـٰهَا عَلَيْهِمْ إِلَّا ٱبْتِغَآءَ رِضْوَٰنِ ٱللَّهِ فَمَا رَعَوْهَا حَقَّ رِعَايَتِهَا ۖ فَـَٔاتَيْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنْهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ ۖ وَكَثِيرٌ مِّنْهُمْ فَـٰسِقُونَ (٢٧)
```

```english
Then We followed them up with Our apostles and We followed [them] with Jesus son of Mary, and We gave him the Evangel, and We put kindness and mercy into the hearts of those who followed him. But as for monasticism, they innovated it—We had not prescribed it for them—only seeking Allah’s pleasure. Yet they did not observe it with due observance. So We gave to the faithful among them their [due] reward, but many of them are transgressors. (27)
```

```arabic
يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱتَّقُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ وَءَامِنُوا۟ بِرَسُولِهِۦ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِن رَّحْمَتِهِۦ وَيَجْعَل لَّكُمْ نُورًا تَمْشُونَ بِهِۦ وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ (٢٨)
```

```english
O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and have faith in His Apostle. He will grant you a double share of His mercy and a light to walk by, and He will forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful; (28)
```

```arabic
لِّئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ أَهْلُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ أَلَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلَىٰ شَىْءٍ مِّن فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ ۙ وَأَنَّ ٱلْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ ٱللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (٢٩)
```

```english
so that the People of the Book may know that they do not control Allah grace in any wise and that in Allah’s hand is all grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes and Allah is dispenser of a mighty grace. (29)
```

## (Commentary)

Subsequently, after the Exalted pointed to the hardness of the hearts of the believers, their sluggishness, and their listlessness in fulfilling religious obligations—especially in spending in the way of Allah, which is the mainstay of the matter of struggle (الجهاد) — *the striving in the way of Allah to establish truth* — and after He likened them to the People of the Book (أهل الكتاب) whose hearts hardened when the term was prolonged for them,

He mentioned that the divine purpose for sending the messengers and sending down the Book and the Balance with them is so that mankind may maintain justice (القسط) — *the equitable distribution of rights and duties* — and live in a just society. He sent down iron to test His servants in defending their righteous society and spreading the word of truth on earth, in addition to the benefits in iron from which they profit.

Then He mentioned that He sent Noah and Abraham (عليه السلام) and placed prophethood (النبوة) and the Book in their progeny, following them with messenger after messenger. Thus, the matter continued in every nation: some were guided and believed, while many of them were transgressors (فاسقون). Finally, He concluded the discourse in the Surah by inviting them to perfect their faith so that they might be given two portions of His mercy.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلَنا بِالْبَيِّناتِ وَأَنْزَلْنا مَعَهُمُ الْكِتابَ وَالْمِيزانَ لِيَقُومَ النَّاسُ بِالْقِسْطِ** — "Certainly We sent Our apostles with manifest proofs, and We sent down with them the Book and the Balance, so that mankind may maintain justice" (57:25), etc. This is a new beginning (استئناف) through which the meaning of the legislation of religion (الدين) — *the divine system of beliefs and laws* — is clarified by the sending of messengers and the revelation of the Book and the Balance. It explains that the purpose of this is for mankind to maintain justice and to be tested by that and by the sending down of iron, so that He may distinguish those who help Allah in the Unseen (الغيب) — *that which lies beyond sensory perception*. It also clarifies that the matter of the message (الرسالة) has remained continuous among people, and that in every nation, some have continued to be guided while many of them are transgressors.

Thus, His saying: **لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا رُسُلَنا بِالْبَيِّناتِ** means with manifest signs (الآيات البينات) by which it becomes clear that they are sent from the side of Allah, the Glorified, consisting of dazzling miracles, clear glad tidings, and decisive proofs.

And His saying: **وَأَنْزَلْنا مَعَهُمُ الْكِتابَ** refers to the revelation (الوحي) which is suitable to be written so that it becomes a book, containing the knowledge of religion in terms of belief and action. These are five: the Book of Noah, the Book of Abraham, the Torah, the Gospel, and the Quran.

And His saying: **وَالْمِيزانَ لِيَقُومَ النَّاسُ بِالْقِسْطِ**. The commentators have interpreted the Balance (الميزان) — *the instrument of measurement and justice* — as the two-scaled balance used to weigh heavy objects. They took His saying: **لِيَقُومَ النَّاسُ بِالْقِسْطِ** as a finality (غاية) related to the sending down of the Balance. The meaning would be:

We sent down the Balance so that people may maintain justice in their transactions, so they do not lose out due to the disruption of weights and the proportions between things. For the stability of human life is through social organization (الاجتماع), and the stability of social organization is through the transactions circulating among them and the exchanges of goods and commodities. The stability of transactions involving weighed items lies in preserving the proportions between them, which is the function of the balance.

However, it is not improbable—and Allah knows best—that the Balance (الميزان) refers to the religion (الدين). For religion is that by which the beliefs and actions of human individuals are weighed, and it is that by which the happy life of people, both collectively and individually, is sustained. This meaning is more harmonious with the context, which addresses the state of the people regarding their humility (خشوع), the hardness of their hearts, and their diligence or negligence in the matter of religion. It has also been said that the Balance here means justice (العدل), and it has been said it means the intellect (العقل).

And His saying: **وَأَنْزَلْنَا الْحَدِيدَ**. It appears that this is like His saying, the Exalted: **وَأَنْزَلَ لَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَنْعامِ ثَمانِيَةَ أَزْواجٍ** — "and He sent down for you eight pairs of livestock" (39:6). It has already been mentioned in the commentary of that verse that naming the creation on earth as "sending down" (إنزال) is only because He, the Exalted, names the appearance of things in the cosmos after they were not as a sending down of them from His treasuries which are with Him, and from the Unseen (الغيب) to the manifest (الشهادة). The Exalted said: **وَإِنْ مِنْ شَيْءٍ إِلَّا عِنْدَنا خَزائِنُهُ وَما نُنَزِّلُهُ إِلَّا بِقَدَرٍ مَعْلُومٍ** — "There is not a thing but its treasuries are with Us, and We do not send it down except in a known measure" (15:21).

And His saying: **فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ وَمَنافِعُ لِلنَّاسِ**. Might (البأس) — *strength and intensity in impact* — is the intensity of effect, and its usage is predominant in the intensity of defense and combat. Wars, battles, and types of defense have never ceased to have a dire need for iron and the various types of weapons made from it since man became aware of it and extracted it.

As for the benefits (المنافع) it contains for mankind, they require no explanation, as it has a role in all branches of life and the industries associated with them.

And His saying: **وَلِيَعْلَمَ اللهُ مَنْ يَنْصُرُهُ وَرُسُلَهُ بِالْغَيْبِ**. This is a finality (غاية) conjoined to an omitted phrase. The estimation is: "We sent down iron for such-and-such and so that Allah may know who helps Him," etc. What is meant by helping Him and His messengers is the struggle (الجهاد) in His way in defense of the religious society and to spread the word of truth. The help being "in the Unseen" (بالغيب) means it occurs while He is absent from them or they are absent from Him. What is meant by His "knowing" those who help Him and His messengers is their being distinguished from those who do not help.

He concluded the verse with His saying: **إِنَّ اللهَ قَوِيٌّ عَزِيزٌ** — "Indeed Allah is all-strong, all-mighty" (57:25). The reason for this was to point out that His commanding them to struggle is only to distinguish the obedient among them from others, not due to any need on the part of the Exalted for a helper to help Him; for He, the Exalted, is All-Strong (قوي), to Whom weakness has no path, and All-Mighty (عزيز), to Whom humiliation has no path.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً وَإِبْراهِيمَ وَجَعَلْنا فِي ذُرِّيَّتِهِمَا النُّبُوَّةَ وَالْكِتابَ فَمِنْهُمْ مُهْتَدٍ وَكَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ** — "Certainly We sent Noah and Abraham and We ordained prophethood and the Book among their descendants. Some of them are [rightly] guided, and many of them are transgressors" (57:26). This is the beginning of an indication that guidance and transgression have been ongoing in past nations until today; no nation has ever been righteous in all its individual members; rather, many of them have always been transgressors.

The pronouns in "among them" (فَمِنْهُمْ) and "among them" (فَمِنْهُمْ) refer to the descendants (الذرية), and the rest is clear.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ثُمَّ قَفَّيْنا عَلى آثارِهِمْ بِرُسُلِنا وَقَفَّيْنا بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَآتَيْناهُ الْإِنْجِيلَ** — "Then We followed them up with Our apostles and We followed [them] up with Jesus son of Mary, and We gave him the Evangel" (57:27). In *al-Majma'*: "Following up" (التقفية) is to place something in the track of another in a continuous manner. For this reason, the endings of poems are called rhymes (قواف) because they follow the verse in its track, continuing into others upon its path. End quote.

The pronoun in "in their tracks" (عَلى آثارِهِمْ) refers to Noah, Abraham, and the predecessors among their descendants. The proof for this is that there is no prophet after Noah except from his descendants, because the lineage after him belongs to him. Furthermore, Jesus is from the descendants of Abraham. The Exalted said regarding Noah: **وَجَعَلْنا ذُرِّيَّتَهُ هُمُ الْباقِينَ** — "and We made his descendants the survivors" (37:77), and He said: **وَمِنْ ذُرِّيَّتِهِ داوُدَ وَسُلَيْمانَ** — "and of his descendants, David and Solomon..." until He said: **وَعِيسى** — "and Jesus" (6:84-85). Thus, what is meant by His saying: **ثُمَّ قَفَّيْنا عَلى آثارِهِمْ بِرُسُلِنا** etc., is the following up with the later ones of their descendants upon the tracks of the two of them and the earlier ones of their descendants.

In His saying: **عَلى آثارِهِمْ** there is an indication that the path traversed is one, in which some follow the track of others.

And His saying: **وَقَفَّيْنا بِعِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَآتَيْناهُ الْإِنْجِيلَ وَجَعَلْنا فِي قُلُوبِ الَّذِينَ اتَّبَعُوهُ رَأْفَةً وَرَحْمَةً** — "and We followed [them] up with Jesus son of Mary, and We gave him the Evangel, and We put kindness and mercy into the hearts of those who followed him" (57:27). Kindness (الرأفة) and mercy (الرحمة) are—according to what has been said—synonymous. It is narrated from some that kindness (الرأفة) is used for warding off evil, and mercy (الرحمة) for bringing about good.

It appears that what is meant by placing kindness and mercy in the hearts of those who followed him is granting them the success (توفيق) for kindness and mercy among themselves, so they lived in mutual support and peace, just as Allah, the Glorified, described those with the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) as having mercy when He said: **رُحَماءُ بَيْنَهُمْ** — "compassionate among themselves" (48:29). It has also been said that placing kindness and mercy in their hearts means commanding them and encouraging them toward these qualities and promising reward for them.

And His saying: **وَرَهْبانِيَّةً ابْتَدَعُوها ما كَتَبْناها عَلَيْهِمْ** — "and monasticism they innovated—We did not prescribe it for them" (57:27). Monasticism (الرهبانية) — *the practice of asceticism and seclusion* — is derived from dread (الرهبة), which is fear. It is conventionally applied to a person's withdrawal from people for the worship of Allah out of fear of Him. Innovation (الابتداع) is to bring about something that was not preceded in religion, tradition, or craft. His saying: **ما كَتَبْناها عَلَيْهِمْ** is in the sense of an answer to a presumed question, as if it were asked: "What is the meaning of their innovating it?" So it was said: "We did not prescribe it for them."

The meaning is that they innovated monasticism from themselves without Us legislating it for them.

And His saying: **إِلَّا ابْتِغاءَ رِضْوانِ اللهِ فَما رَعَوْها حَقَّ رِعايَتِها** — "except to seek Allah’s pleasure; yet they did not observe it with due observance" (57:27). This is a disconnected exception (استثناء منقطع), meaning: We did not impose it upon them, but they established it from themselves seeking the pleasure of Allah and searching for His approval, yet they did not maintain it with its due maintenance by transgressing its limits.

In this is an indication that it was pleasing to Him, the Exalted, even though He did not legislate it; rather, they were the ones who innovated it.

And His saying: **فَآتَيْنَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مِنْهُمْ أَجْرَهُمْ وَكَثِيرٌ مِنْهُمْ فاسِقُونَ** — "So We gave those of them who had faith their reward, and many of them are transgressors" (57:27). This is an indication that they are like the previous nations of the messengers: among them are believers rewarded for their faith, and many of them are transgressors, and transgression is predominant.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَآمِنُوا بِرَسُولِهِ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ** — "O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and have faith in His Apostle. He will grant you a double share of His mercy" (57:28), etc. The command to those who have faith to have godwariness (التقوى) — *the state of being conscious of Allah's presence and avoiding His disobedience* — and faith in the Messenger, despite the fact that those who responded to the call and believed in Allah also believed in His Messenger, is proof that what is meant by faith in the Messenger is complete following and total obedience to His Messenger in what he commands and forbids. This applies whether what he commands or forbids is a legal ruling (حكم شرعي) or issued from him by virtue of his authority (ولاية) over the affairs of the nation, as the Exalted said: **فَلا وَرَبِّكَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ حَتَّى يُحَكِّمُوكَ فِيما شَجَرَ بَيْنَهُمْ ثُمَّ لا يَجِدُوا فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ حَرَجاً مِمَّا قَضَيْتَ وَيُسَلِّمُوا تَسْلِيماً** — "But no, by your Lord! They will not believe until they make you a judge in their disputes, then do not find within their hearts any dissent from your verdict and submit in total submission" (4:65).

So this is faith after faith, and a degree above the degree of faith that might sometimes lack its effect and thus not result in its consequence due to its weakness. In this way, it suits His saying: **يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ**. The portion (الكفل) is the lot and share; thus, he has reward upon reward just as it is faith upon faith.

It has been said: What is meant by giving two portions of mercy is giving them two rewards like the believers of the People of the Book. It is as if it were said: He will give you what was promised to those who believed from the People of the Book of the two rewards, because you are like them in believing in the previous messengers and in their Seal (عليه السلام), not making a distinction between any of His messengers.

And His saying: **وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ نُوراً تَمْشُونَ بِهِ** — "and He will give you a light to walk by" (57:28). It has been said: It means on the Day of Resurrection, and it is the light referred to in His saying: **يَسْعى نُورُهُمْ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمانِهِمْ** — "their light moving swiftly before them and on their right" (57:12).

However, this is a restriction without proof; rather, they have their light in this world, which is indicated by the saying of the Exalted: **أَوَمَنْ كانَ مَيْتاً فَأَحْيَيْناهُ وَجَعَلْنا لَهُ نُوراً يَمْشِي بِهِ فِي النَّاسِ كَمَنْ مَثَلُهُ فِي الظُّلُماتِ لَيْسَ بِخارِجٍ مِنْها** — "Is he who was lifeless, then We gave him life and maintained for him a light by which he walks among the people, like one whose description is that he is in a manifold darkness from which he cannot emerge?" (6:122). And they have their light in the Hereafter, which is indicated by His saying: **يَوْمَ تَرَى الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَالْمُؤْمِناتِ يَسْعى نُورُهُمْ بَيْنَ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَبِأَيْمانِهِمْ** — "The day you will see the faithful men and the faithful women, their light moving swiftly before them and on their right" (57:12), and other verses.

Then the Exalted completed His promise of giving them two portions of His mercy and granting a light to walk by with forgiveness (المغفرة), saying: **وَيَغْفِرْ لَكُمْ وَاللهُ غَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ** — "and He will forgive you, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful" (57:28).

**His saying, the Exalted:** **لِئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ أَهْلُ الْكِتابِ أَلَّا يَقْدِرُونَ عَلى شَيْءٍ مِنْ فَضْلِ اللهِ** — "So that the People of the Book may know that they do not have any power over Allah’s grace" (57:29). The apparent context is that in the verse there is a transition (التفات) from addressing the believers to addressing the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). What is meant by "knowledge" (العلم) is absolute belief, like a claim (الزعم). The word **أَنْ** is the lightened form of the heavy one. The pronoun in "they have power" (يَقْدِرُونَ) refers to the believers. The speech is a justification for the content of the previous verse.

The meaning is: We only commanded them with faith after faith and promised them two portions of mercy and the granting of light and forgiveness so that the People of the Book would not believe that the believers have no power over anything of Allah's grace, unlike the believers from the People of the Book who are given their reward twice because they believed.

It has been said that the **لا** in **لِئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ** is extra, and the pronoun in **يَقْدِرُونَ** refers to the People of the Book. The meaning would be: We only promised the believers what We promised so that the People of the Book—who say: "Whoever among us believes in your Book has two rewards, and whoever does not believe has one reward for his belief in our Book"—may know that they have no power over anything of Allah's grace if they do not believe. This, as is not hidden from you, involves affectation (التكلف).

And His saying: **وَأَنَّ الْفَضْلَ بِيَدِ اللهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَنْ يَشاءُ وَاللهُ ذُو الْفَضْلِ الْعَظِيمِ** — "and that grace is in Allah’s hand—He gives it to whomever He wishes and Allah is the dispenser of a great grace" (57:29). This is conjoined to "that they may [not] know." The meaning is: We only promised what We promised because of such-and-such, and because grace is in the hand of Allah, and Allah is the Possessor of Great Grace.

There are other opinions and possibilities regarding the verse, but there is no benefit in citing or discussing them.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *Jawami' al-Jami'*, it is narrated: "Gabriel descended with the Balance and gave it to Noah (عليه السلام) and said: 'Command your people to weigh with it.'"

In *al-Ihtijaj*, from Ali (عليه السلام) in a long tradition, he said: "**وَأَنْزَلْنَا الْحَدِيدَ فِيهِ بَأْسٌ شَدِيدٌ** — 'and We sent down iron, wherein is great might' (57:25); His sending it down is His creating it."

In *al-Majma'*, from Ibn Mas'ud, who said: "I was riding behind the Messenger of Allah on a donkey, and he said: 'O son of Umm 'Abd, do you know from where the Children of Israel innovated monasticism?' I said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'Tyrants appeared over them after Jesus (عليه السلام) who acted in disobedience to Allah. The people of faith became angry and fought them. The people of faith were defeated three times until only a few of them remained.

They said: "If we appear before these people, they will annihilate us, and no one will remain for the religion to call to it. So come, let us disperse in the land until Allah sends the Prophet whom Jesus promised us"—meaning Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم). So they dispersed in the caves (غيران) of the mountains and innovated monasticism. Some of them held fast to their religion, and some of them disbelieved.' Then he recited this verse: **وَرَهْبانِيَّةً ابْتَدَعُوها ما كَتَبْناها عَلَيْهِمْ** — 'and monasticism they innovated—We did not prescribe it for them' (57:27) to the end of it.

Then he said: 'O son of Umm 'Abd, do you know what the monasticism of my nation is?' I said: 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He said: 'Migration (الهجرة), struggle (الجهاد), prayer (الصلاة), fasting (الصوم), pilgrimage (الحج), and the minor pilgrimage (العمرة).'"

In *al-Kafi*, with its chain of narration from Abu al-Jarud, who said: "I said to Abu Ja'far (عليه السلام): 'Allah has given the People of the Book much good.' He said: 'And what is that?' I said: 'The saying of Allah, Mighty and Majestic: **الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ هُمْ بِهِ يُؤْمِنُونَ** — "Those to whom We gave the Book before it, they believe in it" (28:52) until His saying: **أُولئِكَ يُؤْتَوْنَ أَجْرَهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ بِما صَبَرُوا** — "Those will be given their reward twice for their patience" (28:54).' He said: 'Allah has given you as He gave them.' Then he recited: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَآمِنُوا بِرَسُولِهِ يُؤْتِكُمْ كِفْلَيْنِ مِنْ رَحْمَتِهِ وَيَجْعَلْ لَكُمْ نُوراً تَمْشُونَ بِهِ** — "O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and have faith in His Apostle. He will grant you a double share of His mercy and He will give you a light to walk by" (57:28), meaning an Imam whom you follow.'"

In *al-Majma'*, from Sa'id ibn Jubayr: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) sent Ja'far with seventy riders to the Negus to invite him. He came to him and invited him, and he responded and believed in him. When it was time for his departure, some people from his kingdom who had believed in him—and they were forty men—said: 'Permit us to go to this Prophet and submit with him.'

So they came with Ja'far. When they saw the destitution (الخصاصة) of the Muslims, they asked permission from the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and said: 'O Prophet of Allah, we have wealth, and we see the destitution of the Muslims. If you permit us, we will return and bring our wealth and share it with the Muslims.' He permitted them, so they returned and brought their wealth and shared it with the Muslims. Then Allah revealed concerning them: **الَّذِينَ آتَيْناهُمُ الْكِتابَ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ هُمْ بِهِ يُؤْمِنُونَ** — 'Those to whom We gave the Book before it, they believe in it' (28:52) until His saying: **وَمِمَّا رَزَقْناهُمْ يُنْفِقُونَ** — 'and spend out of what We have provided them' (28:54). This was the spending with which they shared with the Muslims.

When the People of the Book who did not believe in him heard His saying: **أُولئِكَ يُؤْتَوْنَ أَجْرَهُمْ مَرَّتَيْنِ بِما صَبَرُوا** — 'Those will be given their reward twice for their patience' (28:54), they boasted over the Muslims and said: 'O assembly of Muslims, as for those of us who believe in our Book and your Book, they have two rewards; and those of us who believe in our Book have a reward like your rewards. So what is your merit over us?' Then His saying was revealed: **يا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا اتَّقُوا اللهَ وَآمِنُوا بِرَسُولِهِ** — 'O you who have faith! Be wary of Allah and have faith in His Apostle' (57:28) the verse. So He granted them two rewards and increased for them the light and forgiveness. Then He said: **لِئَلَّا يَعْلَمَ أَهْلُ الْكِتابِ** — 'So that the People of the Book may know' (57:29)."
